title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens completed his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points in 34 games.
Hagens, a top prospect, may turn professional soon and join the Bruins as a late-season addition.
The Bruins' GM, Don Sweeney, is open to discussions about integrating prospects like Hagens into the lineup.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Bradley Cooper directs Will Arnett in the comedy-drama 'Is This Thing On?', now streaming on Disney+.
'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' features Cillian Murphy and Barry Keoghan, available on Netflix.
'Born to Bowl', a docuseries narrated by Liev Schreiber, streams on HBO Max.

title: ‘We’ll see’: Alex Cora mum on future with Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora is focused on the present as he nears the end of his Red Sox contract in 2027.
Cora needs 134 more wins to surpass Terry Francona for second-most managerial wins in Red Sox history.
Cora's future decisions are influenced by his family's needs, including his sons' education.

title: The Bruins’ second line isn’t just a pleasant surprise. They’ve been one of the NHL’s best this year.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins' second line of Viktor Arvidsson, Pavel Zacha, and Casey Mittelstadt has outscored opponents 35-16 this season.
The line's +19 goal differential ranks second in the NHL, only behind the Colorado Avalanche's top line at +21.
Pavel Zacha leads the second line with a career-high 23 goals and 51 points in 66 games this season.

title: He’s running Boston to support kids and families at MGH
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Andrew Mahr, 37, from Needham, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support MGH pediatric cancer care.
This is Mahr's second time running with MGH, inspired by the courage of young patients he saw in 2014.
Mahr highlights the urgency of supporting pediatric cancer research, which receives less funding than adult cancer studies.

title: ‘More money in my pocket is a no-brainer’: Readers weigh in on proposed income tax cut
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A proposal to reduce Massachusetts' income tax rate from 5% to 4% could reduce state revenue by $5.1 billion annually.
A Boston.com poll indicated that 78% of respondents support the tax cut proposition for the November ballot, while 20% oppose it.
Governor Maura Healey opposes the measure, warning it may harm state finances and impact economic competitiveness.

title: A voodoo doll, a love triangle, and an alleged poisoning: Testimony begins in Salisbury antifreeze murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judy Church, 67, is on trial for allegedly murdering her boyfriend Leroy Fowler with antifreeze amid a love triangle.
Prosecutors claim Church joked about poisoning Fowler and found her DNA on a Powerade bottle containing ethylene glycol.
The defense argues the relationship was tense but insists Church filmed Fowler to show substance abuse effects, not to harm him.

title: Healey pushes to get rid of Sunday hunting ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Governor Maura Healey proposes legislation to lift the Sunday hunting ban in Massachusetts and allow widespread crossbow use.
MassWildlife supports changes to address deer overpopulation; only five of 15 zones are within density goals.
Proposed legislation includes reducing bowhunter setback distances to 250 feet, potentially opening 40,000 acres for hunting.

title: Cape Cod magnet fisher pulls ‘pristine-looking’ derringer pistol from Bridgewater river
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Nate DeMontigny found a pristine derringer pistol sealed in a case while magnet fishing in a Bridgewater river.
The pistol, appearing brand new, drew online attention, amassing 1.2 million views in two days on Reddit.
DeMontigny contacted police upon finding the weapon, highlighting his commitment to safety during magnet fishing.

title: Teen charged with murder after stabbing in Chelsea
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old, Sergio Josue Castellanos, is charged with murder after a Chelsea stabbing left one dead.
Police found multiple stabbing victims near Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue in Chelsea at 6:45 p.m.
The 25-year-old victim died from stab wounds after surgery; other victim details are undisclosed.
